title: Config
description: Top-level configuration defining registries, mappings, and sync behavior.

defaults.description: Default source, targets, tags, and platforms inherited by all mappings.

global.description: Global engine settings applied across all syncs.

mappings.description: Image mapping rules defining what to sync and where.

registries.description: Named registry definitions keyed by alias.

target_groups.description: Named groups of registry aliases for multi-target fanout.

ArtifactsConfig.description:
Configuration for OCI artifact (signatures, SBOMs, attestations) sync.

Controls whether referrers are discovered and transferred alongside their parent image manifests.

enabled.description: Whether artifact sync is enabled (default: true).

exclude.description: Exclude artifacts whose artifact type matches one of these MIME types.

include.description: Only sync artifacts whose artifact type matches one of these MIME types.

require_artifacts.description: When true, every synced image must have at least one referrer. Images without referrers cause a sync failure instead of silently producing unsigned images at the target.

DefaultsConfig.description: Default values inherited by all mappings unless individually overridden.

artifacts.description: Artifact sync configuration applied to all mappings unless overridden.

platforms.description:
Platform filter applied to all mappings unless overridden.

Each entry must be `os/arch` or `os/arch/variant` (e.g. `linux/amd64`, `linux/arm/v7`).

source.description: Default source registry alias for all mappings.

tags.description: Default tag filter rules inherited by mappings without their own.

targets.description: Default target registry alias or group for all mappings.

GlobOrList.description: A glob pattern: either a single string or a list of patterns.

GlobalConfig.description: Global engine settings that apply across all sync operations.

cache_dir.description:
Cache directory for persistent cache and blob staging.

Defaults to a directory next to the config file when not specified.

cache_ttl.description:
Warm cache TTL as a human-readable duration (e.g. "12h", "30m").

`"0"` disables TTL-based expiry (cache never expires by age; lazy invalidation only). Defaults to `"12h"` when not specified.

staging_size_limit.description:
Disk staging size limit as a human-readable size (e.g. "2GB", "500MB").

Uses SI decimal prefixes: 1 GB = 1,000,000,000 bytes. `0` disables disk staging. When absent, no eviction is performed.

MappingConfig.description: A single image mapping rule: source repo, target registries, and tag filters.

artifacts.description: Artifact sync configuration for this mapping, overriding `defaults.artifacts`.

platforms.description:
Platform filter for this mapping, overriding any value in `defaults`.

Each entry must be `os/arch` or `os/arch/variant` (e.g. `linux/amd64`, `linux/arm/v7`).

tags.description: Tag filter rules for this mapping, overriding `defaults.tags`.

targets.description: Target registry alias or group, overriding `defaults.targets`.

to.description: Destination repository path; defaults to `from` when absent.

RegistryConfig.description: Per-registry settings: URL, auth method, concurrency, and credentials.

auth_type.description: Authentication method to use for this registry.

aws_profile.description:
Named AWS profile for ECR credential resolution.

When set, ECR auth for this registry uses `aws_config::ConfigLoader::profile_name(p)` instead of the ambient credential chain. Only valid with explicit `auth_type: ecr`.

Use this when the registry requires credentials distinct from the ambient identity (for example, a third-party ECR accessed with static IAM-user keys while the ambient chain serves the rest of the workload).

head_first.description:
HEAD-check targets before pulling full source manifests on cache miss.

When enabled, the engine issues a manifest HEAD against all targets before performing a full source manifest GET. If every target already holds the same digest as the source HEAD, the expensive GET is skipped. This conserves rate-limit tokens on source registries with aggressive quotas (e.g., Docker Hub).

max_concurrent.description:
Per-registry aggregate concurrency cap (default: 50).

Limits the total number of simultaneous in-flight HTTP requests to this registry across all action types. This is independent of the global `max_concurrent_transfers` (which caps image-level parallelism).

token.description: Bearer token for static token auth (`auth_type: static_token`).

SemverPrerelease.description: How to handle semver pre-release tags.

SortOrder.description: Sort order for the final tag list.

TagsConfig.description: Tag filter and selection rules for a mapping.

exclude.description: Exclude tags matching one or more glob patterns.

glob.description: Include tags matching one or more glob patterns.

immutable_tags.description:
Glob pattern for immutable tags (e.g. `"v?[0-9]*.[0-9]*.[0-9]*"`).

When a tag matches this pattern AND already exists in the target's tag list, the tag is skipped with zero API calls. Semver tags are conventionally immutable; this avoids redundant HEAD checks.

latest.description: Keep only the N most recent tags after sorting.

min_tags.description: Minimum number of tags to retain regardless of filters.

semver.description: Include tags matching a semver range (e.g. `>=1.0, <2.0`).

semver_prerelease.description: Whether to include or exclude semver pre-release tags.

TargetsValue.description: Target specification: either a named group or an inline list of registry aliases.

anyOf.description: A single target group name defined in `target_groups`.
An inline list of registry aliases.
